{'rubric_id': 'ri_2', 'reason': 'Warn-threshold logic in memo deviates from rubric: uses global 8% of weighted_driver_sum rather than 8% of largest driver per record.', 'recommended_fix': 'Change warn calculation to 0.08 * MAX(metric1,metric2,metric3) per record and show unit tests.'}

planned_changes: Update workbook warn-threshold formula to compute per-record as 0.08 * MAX(metric1, metric2, metric3). Update all dependent flags (warn_flag) and the memo text to match.

Export a CSV of all computed columns (driver1, driver2, driver3, weighted_driver_sum, margin, warn_flag, fail_flag, needs_review, heat_risk_reduction_value, risk_rank, per_parcel_cost, cumulative_funded_cost, funded_flag). Name file computed_columns.csv and include in artifacts.

Produce a plain-text formula sheet (formula_sheet.txt) listing the exact Excel formulas/cell templates used for each computed column and any named ranges. Embed any helper formulas used for ranking and cumulative funding checks.

Add a verification_readme.md that documents how computed_columns.csv and formula_sheet.txt were generated (script/command, timestamp) and include a minimal run log or screenshot proving the workbook was opened/written.
Add explicit per-parcel cost and cumulative funded-cost numbers to the planning_brief recommendation table and include one-line justification per funded parcel.
Add 'unit check' appendix to the planning brief showing expected vs actual results for R1-R4 (margin, warn/fail/needs_review flags) and include these rows in computed_columns.csv.
If tree_canopy_pct is transformed (100 - tree_canopy_pct) for ranking, add a short rationale and a sensitivity appendix showing ranking with and without the transformation.

Resolve reporting inconsistencies: update the deliverable metadata to clearly state deterministic precheck results, model semantic scores per rubric item, and the aggregation method used to produce final_score.
raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Fix warn-threshold logic that diverges from rubric, provide verifiable exports and reproduction materials so graders can validate formulas without openpyxl, and resolve internal score/reporting inconsistencies.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Fix warn-threshold logic that diverges from rubric, provide verifiable exports and reproduction materials so graders can validate formulas without openpyxl, and resolve internal score/reporting inconsistencies.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Artifacts present and planning_brief.md is thorough (methods, sensitivity, limitations, and recommendation). Two main deficiencies: (1) warn-threshold logic in the memo appears inconsistent with the rubric (memo uses a global 8% of weighted_driver_sum; rubric requires 8% of the largest driver per record), and (2) the workbook's formulas and enforcement claims could not be independently verified because the .xlsx could not be programmatically inspected (openpyxl unavailable). Additional internal inconsistencies across grader/verifier outputs (failed_criteria vs. deterministic precheck and score aggregation) reduce report clarity and traceability.
